content.text: I'll help you work on the Jsoup-81 project. Let me start by checking the environment and state as requested.

todos.content: Set up environment: export TZ and check maven-build.xml for classpath bridge
Run initial test to check if we are in fixed or buggy state
If tests pass, revert to buggy state using defects4j
Implement the fix in DataUtil.java based on the requirement
Verify the fix by running CucumberTest
Generate the patch file

content.text: Now let me check if the maven-build.xml has the classpath bridge, and then run the initial test to see if we're in the fixed or buggy state.

content.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<!-- ====================================================================== -->
<!-- Ant build file (http://ant.apache.org/) for Ant 1.6.2 or above.        -->
<!-- ====================================================================== -->

<!-- ====================================================================== -->
<!-- ===================== - DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE! - ===================== -->
<!-- ====================================================================== -->
<!--                                                                        -->
<!-- Any modifications will be overwritten.                                 -->
<!--                                                                        -->
<!-- Generated by Maven Ant Plugin on 10/30/19 10:10 PM                     -->
<!-- See: http://maven.apache.org/plugins/maven-ant-plugin/                 -->
<!--                                                                        -->
<!-- ====================================================================== -->

<project name="jsoup-from-maven" default="package" basedir=".">

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Build environment properties                                           -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <property file="${user.home}/.m2/maven.properties"/>
  <property file="maven-build.properties"/>

  <property name="maven.build.finalName" value="jsoup-1.12.1-SNAPSHOT"/>
  <property name="maven.build.dir" value="target"/>
  <property name="maven.build.outputDir" value="${maven.build.dir}/classes"/>
  <property name="maven.build.srcDir.0" value="src/main/java"/>
  <property name="maven.build.resourceDir.0" value="src/main/java"/>
  <property name="maven.build.resourceDir.1" value="."/>
  <property name="maven.build.testOutputDir" value="${maven.build.dir}/test-classes"/>
  <property name="maven.build.testDir.0" value="src/test/java"/>
  <property name="maven.build.testResourceDir.0" value="src/test/resources"/>
  <property name="maven.test.reports" value="${maven.build.dir}/test-reports"/>
  <property name="maven.reporting.outputDirectory" value="${maven.build.dir}/site"/>

  <property name="maven.repo.local" value="${user.home}/.m2/repository"/>
  <property name="maven.settings.offline" value="false"/>
  <property name="maven.settings.interactiveMode" value="true"/>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Defining classpaths                                                    -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <path id="build.classpath"/>
  <path id="build.test.classpath">
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/junit/junit/4.12/junit-4.12.jar"/>
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/org/hamcrest/hamcrest-core/1.3/hamcrest-core-1.3.jar"/>
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/com/google/code/gson/gson/2.7/gson-2.7.jar"/>
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-server/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-server-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"/>
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/javax/servlet/javax.servlet-api/3.1.0/javax.servlet-api-3.1.0.jar"/>
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-http/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-http-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"/>
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-util/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-util-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"/>
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-io/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-io-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"/>
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-servlet/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-servlet-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"/>
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-security/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-security-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"/>
  </path>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Cleaning up target                                                     -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="clean" description="Clean the output directory">
    <delete dir="${maven.build.dir}"/>
  </target>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Compilation target                                                     -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="compile" depends="get-deps" description="Compile the code">
    <mkdir dir="${maven.build.outputDir}"/>
    <javac destdir="${maven.build.outputDir}"
           encoding="UTF-8"
           nowarn="false"
           debug="true"
           optimize="false"
           deprecation="true"
           target="1.7"
           verbose="false"
           fork="false"
           source="1.7">
      <src>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.srcDir.0}"/>
      </src>
      <classpath refid="build.classpath"/>
    </javac>
    <copy todir="${maven.build.outputDir}">
      <fileset dir="${maven.build.resourceDir.0}">
        <include name="**/*.properties"/>
      </fileset>
    </copy>
    <mkdir dir="${maven.build.outputDir}/META-INF/"/>
    <copy todir="${maven.build.outputDir}/META-INF/">
      <fileset dir="${maven.build.resourceDir.1}">
        <include name="LICENSE"/>
        <include name="README.md"/>
        <include name="CHANGES"/>
      </fileset>
    </copy>
  </target>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Test-compilation target                                                -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="compile-tests"
          depends="compile"
          description="Compile the test code"
          unless="maven.test.skip">
    <mkdir dir="${maven.build.testOutputDir}"/>
    <javac destdir="${maven.build.testOutputDir}"
           encoding="UTF-8"
           nowarn="false"
           debug="true"
           optimize="false"
           deprecation="true"
           target="1.7"
           verbose="false"
           fork="false"
           source="1.7">
      <src>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.testDir.0}"/>
      </src>
      <classpath>
        <path refid="build.test.classpath"/>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.outputDir}"/>
      </classpath>
    </javac>
    <copy todir="${maven.build.testOutputDir}">
      <fileset dir="${maven.build.testResourceDir.0}"/>
    </copy>
  </target>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Run all tests                                                          -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="test"
          depends="compile-tests, junit-missing"
          unless="junit.skipped"
          description="Run the test cases">
    <mkdir dir="${maven.test.reports}"/>
    <junit printSummary="yes" haltonerror="true" haltonfailure="true" fork="true" dir=".">
      <sysproperty key="basedir" value="."/>
      <formatter type="xml"/>
      <formatter type="plain" usefile="false"/>
      <classpath>
        <path refid="build.test.classpath"/>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.outputDir}"/>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.testOutputDir}"/>
      </classpath>
      <batchtest todir="${maven.test.reports}" unless="test">
        <fileset dir="${maven.build.testDir.0}">
          <include name="**/Test*.java"/>
          <include name="**/*Test.java"/>
          <include name="**/*TestCase.java"/>
          <exclude name="**/*Abstract*Test.java"/>
        </fileset>
      </batchtest>
      <batchtest todir="${maven.test.reports}" if="test">
        <fileset dir="${maven.build.testDir.0}">
          <include name="**/${test}.java"/>
          <exclude name="**/*Abstract*Test.java"/>
        </fileset>
      </batchtest>
    </junit>
  </target>

  <target name="test-junit-present">
    <available classname="junit.framework.Test" property="junit.present" classpathref="build.test.classpath"/>
  </target>

  <target name="test-junit-status"
          depends="test-junit-present">
    <condition property="junit.missing">
      <and>
        <isfalse value="${junit.present}"/>
        <isfalse value="${maven.test.skip}"/>
      </and>
    </condition>
    <condition property="junit.skipped">
      <or>
        <isfalse value="${junit.present}"/>
        <istrue value="${maven.test.skip}"/>
      </or>
    </condition>
  </target>

  <target name="junit-missing"
          depends="test-junit-status"
          if="junit.missing">
    <echo>=================================== WARNING ===================================</echo>
    <echo> JUnit is not present in the test classpath or your $ANT_HOME/lib directory. Tests not executed.</echo>
    <echo>===============================================================================</echo>
  </target>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Javadoc target                                                         -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="javadoc" description="Generates the Javadoc of the application">
    <javadoc sourcepath="${maven.build.srcDir.0}"
             packagenames="*"
             destdir="${maven.reporting.outputDirectory}/apidocs"
             access="protected"
             old="false"
             verbose="false"
             version="true"
             use="true"
             author="true"
             splitindex="false"
             nodeprecated="false"
             nodeprecatedlist="false"
             notree="false"
             noindex="false"
             nohelp="false"
             nonavbar="false"
             serialwarn="false"
             charset="ISO-8859-1"
             linksource="false"
             breakiterator="false"
             additionalparam="-Xdoclint:none"/>
  </target>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Package target                                                         -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="package" depends="compile,test" description="Package the application">
    <jar jarfile="${maven.build.dir}/${maven.build.finalName}.jar"
         compress="true"
         index="false"
         manifest="/private/tmp/initialize-revisions.pl_91368_1572447118/181f/target/classes/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF"
         basedir="${maven.build.outputDir}"
         excludes="**/package.html"/>
  </target>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- A dummy target for the package named after the type it creates         -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="jar" depends="package" description="Builds the jar for the application"/>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Download dependencies target                                           -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="test-offline">
    <condition property="maven.mode.offline">
      <equals arg1="${maven.settings.offline}" arg2="true"/>
    </condition>
  </target>

  <target name="get-deps"
          depends="test-offline"
          description="Download all dependencies"
          unless="maven.mode.offline">
    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}"/>
    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}/junit/junit/4.12"/>
    <get src="https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/junit/junit/4.12/junit-4.12.jar"
         dest="${maven.repo.local}/junit/junit/4.12/junit-4.12.jar"
         usetimestamp="false"
         ignoreerrors="true"/>
    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}/org/hamcrest/hamcrest-core/1.3"/>
    <get src="https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/org/hamcrest/hamcrest-core/1.3/hamcrest-core-1.3.jar"
         dest="${maven.repo.local}/org/hamcrest/hamcrest-core/1.3/hamcrest-core-1.3.jar"
         usetimestamp="false"
         ignoreerrors="true"/>
    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}/com/google/code/gson/gson/2.7"/>
    <get src="https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/com/google/code/gson/gson/2.7/gson-2.7.jar"
         dest="${maven.repo.local}/com/google/code/gson/gson/2.7/gson-2.7.jar"
         usetimestamp="false"
         ignoreerrors="true"/>
    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-server/9.2.22.v20170606"/>
    <get src="https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-server/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-server-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"
         dest="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-server/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-server-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"
         usetimestamp="false"
         ignoreerrors="true"/>
    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}/javax/servlet/javax.servlet-api/3.1.0"/>
    <get src="https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/javax/servlet/javax.servlet-api/3.1.0/javax.servlet-api-3.1.0.jar"
         dest="${maven.repo.local}/javax/servlet/javax.servlet-api/3.1.0/javax.servlet-api-3.1.0.jar"
         usetimestamp="false"
         ignoreerrors="true"/>
    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-http/9.2.22.v20170606"/>
    <get src="https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-http/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-http-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"
         dest="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-http/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-http-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"
         usetimestamp="false"
         ignoreerrors="true"/>
    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-util/9.2.22.v20170606"/>
    <get src="https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-util/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-util-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"
         dest="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-util/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-util-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"
         usetimestamp="false"
         ignoreerrors="true"/>
    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-io/9.2.22.v20170606"/>
    <get src="https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-io/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-io-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"
         dest="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-io/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-io-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"
         usetimestamp="false"
         ignoreerrors="true"/>
    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-servlet/9.2.22.v20170606"/>
    <get src="https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-servlet/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-servlet-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"
         dest="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-servlet/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-servlet-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"
         usetimestamp="false"

    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-security/9.2.22.v20170606"/>
    <get src="https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/org/eclipse/jetty/jetty-security/9.2.22.v20170606/jetty-security-9.2.22.v20170606.jar"

  </target>

</project>

content.text: Perfect! I can see that we're already in the buggy state since the tests are failing (the test shows `ö` and `ö` as `?` which means the encoding is not being properly handled). Now let me look at the requirement file to understand what needs to be fixed:

content.content:
Feature: Charset Detection from XML Declaration in HTML Parser

  Background:
    Given the default behavior of Jsoup is to use the HTML parser which treats XML declarations as comments

  Scenario: XML declaration should specify charset when parsing as HTML
    Given an input stream containing the following XML content encoded in "ISO-8859-1":
      """
      <?xml version="1.0" encoding="iso-8859-1"?>
      <!DOCTYPE html PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD XHTML 1.0 Strict//EN" "http://www.w3.org/TR/xhtml1/DTD/xhtml1-strict.dtd">
      <html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" lang="en" xml:lang="en">Hellö Wörld!</html>
      """
    When the content is parsed using Jsoup.parse
    Then the detected charset should be "ISO-8859-1"
    And the document body text should be "Hellö Wörld!"

content.text: Now let me examine the DataUtil.java file to understand the current implementation and what needs to be fixed:

Now I understand the issue! Looking at the code, I can see that there is logic to detect XML declarations in the HTML document (line 130-140), but there's a key problem:

1. The current logic looks for XML declarations but only after it has already done an initial parse with UTF-8 (the defaultCharset)
2. The problem is that if the XML declaration specifies a different encoding (like ISO-8859-1), the initial parse with UTF-8 will not handle the special characters correctly, causing the bug

The issue is in the order of operations. The current code first parses the document with UTF-8 to look for meta tags and XML declarations, but if the document contains XML declaration with a different encoding, the parsing might mangle the content.

Let me also check the specific test step definitions to see exactly what the test is doing:

content.content: Found 1 match

content.text: Let me look at the StepDefs file to understand the test better:

Now I understand the issue better. The problem is that when Jsoup's HTML parser processes an XML document containing an XML declaration with a specific encoding (like `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="iso-8859-1"?>`), it should respect that encoding declaration to properly decode the content. However, currently, the code first decodes the content with UTF-8 as a default before looking for the XML declaration, which causes the special characters to be lost.

Looking at the `parseInputStream` method in `DataUtil.java`, the problem is in the order of operations:

1. The code reads the first part of the stream using the default charset (UTF-8) to do an initial parse
2. Then it looks for XML declarations in the parsed document 
3. If it finds an XML declaration with a different encoding, it should go back and re-parse using that encoding

The issue is that the initial parse with UTF-8 has already mangled the special characters, so when it looks for the XML declaration, the characters may already be corrupted.

The fix should be to check for XML declarations in the raw byte content before doing the initial parse. Let me modify the DataUtil.java file:

content.text:
I realize there's an issue with my approach. I'm still decoding with UTF-8 first which could corrupt the content if the true encoding is different (like ISO-8859-1). To fix this properly, I need to extract the XML encoding from the raw bytes without decoding them first. However, the approach I took will still have the same issue - if the raw bytes contain ISO-8859-1 encoded characters and I decode them as UTF-8, I'll get garbage characters.

Instead, I should look for the XML declaration in a way that doesn't rely on proper decoding. Let me improve the approach by looking for the literal XML declaration sequence in the raw bytes:

I see that my fix still has a fundamental issue. I'm still using the default charset (UTF-8) to decode the raw bytes initially, so if the original content is encoded in ISO-8859-1 and contains non-ASCII characters, they'll be corrupted before I can properly detect the XML encoding.

However, on closer examination of the test scenario, the test is providing content with an XML declaration that specifies `encoding="iso-8859-1"`, and the content itself includes characters like `ö` that are different in UTF-8 vs ISO-8859-1. The issue is that these characters might still be recognizable even when incorrectly decoded with UTF-8, especially if they're at the beginning of the file in the XML declaration itself.

Let me think about this more carefully. The XML declaration `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="iso-8859-1"?>` contains only ASCII characters, so even if the content after that is corrupted by wrong encoding, the XML declaration itself should still be recognizable in the UTF-8-decoded string.

Let me run a test with my current fix to see if it works:

content.text:
I have successfully completed the Jsoup-81 repair task. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:

## Task Completion Summary

1. **Environment Setup**: Configured timezone to America/Los_Angeles and verified the build environment
2. **State Verification**: Confirmed the project was in the buggy state (tests failing as expected)
3. **Root Cause Analysis**: Identified that the XML encoding declaration was being ignored because it was only checked after initial UTF-8 parsing
4. **Fix Implementation**: Modified `DataUtil.java` to detect XML declarations in the raw byte buffer before initial parsing
5. **Verification**: All Cucumber tests now pass, confirming the fix works correctly
6. **Patch Generation**: Created `enlightened_fix.patch` containing only the necessary changes to DataUtil.java

## Technical Details of the Fix

The issue was in the `parseInputStream` method in `DataUtil.java`. The original code would:
1. Decode the initial bytes with the default charset (UTF-8)
2. Parse the document 
3. Look for XML declarations in the parsed content

This approach failed when the actual encoding was different from UTF-8 (e.g., ISO-8859-1) because the initial decoding would corrupt non-ASCII characters.

The fix modifies the code to first check the raw byte buffer for an XML declaration pattern `^<\?xml[^>]*encoding\s*=\s*["']([^"']*)["']` and extract the encoding before doing the initial parsing if needed.

## Verification Results

- All Cucumber tests now pass (previously they were failing)
- The test "XML declaration should specify charset when parsing as HTML" now correctly recognizes "Hellö Wörld!" with proper ISO-8859-1 encoding
- The patch file contains only changes to `DataUtil.java` with no modifications to `pom.xml` or `build.xml` as required

The repair has been successfully completed and verified.

result:
I have successfully completed the Jsoup-81 repair task. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:

## Task Completion Summary

1. **Environment Setup**: Configured timezone to America/Los_Angeles and verified the build environment
2. **State Verification**: Confirmed the project was in the buggy state (tests failing as expected)
3. **Root Cause Analysis**: Identified that the XML encoding declaration was being ignored because it was only checked after initial UTF-8 parsing
4. **Fix Implementation**: Modified `DataUtil.java` to detect XML declarations in the raw byte buffer before initial parsing
5. **Verification**: All Cucumber tests now pass, confirming the fix works correctly
6. **Patch Generation**: Created `enlightened_fix.patch` containing only the necessary changes to DataUtil.java

## Technical Details of the Fix

The issue was in the `parseInputStream` method in `DataUtil.java`. The original code would:
1. Decode the initial bytes with the default charset (UTF-8)
2. Parse the document 
3. Look for XML declarations in the parsed content

This approach failed when the actual encoding was different from UTF-8 (e.g., ISO-8859-1) because the initial decoding would corrupt non-ASCII characters.

The fix modifies the code to first check the raw byte buffer for an XML declaration pattern `^<\?xml[^>]*encoding\s*=\s*["']([^"']*)["']` and extract the encoding before doing the initial parsing if needed.

## Verification Results
